method: multi-agent verify + adversarial falsification pass (fresh contexts); direct WebFetch of ai.meta.com and vals.ai, independent search for pricing corroboration
origin_items: Meta AI Blog (Tier 0 html sweep) — Introducing Muse Spark 1.1
data/raw/2026-07-09/techcrunch-ai.json — Meta enters the crowded AI coding battle with Muse Spark 1.1
data/raw/2026-07-09/the-verge-ai.json — Meta says its new AI model is ready to compete on coding

claims.text: Meta released Muse Spark 1.1 on 9 July 2026, multimodal agentic model, 1M-token context, US-only public preview
Pricing $1.25/$4.25 per million tokens, $20 free credits, undercuts rivals
claims.reasoning: Confirmed via Meta's developer blog plus independent Tier-2 corroboration (TechCrunch, CNBC, The Decoder all report matching figures).

claims.text: Meta's 'ready to compete' framing and self-reported benchmark numbers
claims.reasoning: Neither TechCrunch nor The Verge independently tested the model; both relay Meta's own messaging. Meta's own benchmark charts do not count as independent confirmation per policy even when press repeats them.

Initial verifier cited figures (4th of 23, 4/12 sub-benchmarks) that the adversarial pass found did NOT match a direct fetch of the live vals.ai page. Adversarial pass re-fetched vals.ai directly and obtained corrected, verified figures: 5th of 35 models, leading 3 of 27 sub-benchmarks (MedScribe, TaxEval v2, Harvey's Legal Agent Benchmark -- none coding-specific). Published article uses these corrected, directly-verified numbers rather than the initial verifier's figures. Vals AI is confirmed to be a real, legitimate third-party benchmark org, not hallucinated.

claims.text: Zuckerberg's X post, first in ~3 years
claims.reasoning: Initial verifier flagged single-source (TechCrunch only); adversarial pass found independent corroboration from additional outlets (americanbazaaronline, stocktwits, cryptobriefing, inc.com) reporting the same detail and post content, clearing the 2-independent-source bar.

notes: IMPORTANT correction during adversarial review: the verifier's first-pass Vals AI figures (4th of 23 models, 4/12 sub-benchmarks) were wrong -- did not match the live page. Corrected before publication via a direct re-fetch. This is a reminder that live third-party leaderboards drift between snapshots and any cited ranking needs a direct fetch at (or very near) publication time, not a recalled/summarized figure. vals.ai is not yet in policy/sources.yaml -- proposed as a candidate (see source_candidates.json).
